name: Susqueda Reservoir and Rupit Loop Trail
name_original: Espai Natural de Les Guilleries – circular por el Pantà de Susqueda y el Salt de Sallent
description: This difficult 33.5-mile mountain biking route in Les Guilleries offers stunning views of Pantà de Susqueda and the impressive Salt de Salle
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Pantà de Susqueda are situated in Catalonia, Spain, within the Guilleries Mountains and Collsacabra Massif. This region is characterized by lush vegetation, mountainous terrain, and the expansive Susqueda Reservoir formed by the Ter River. The landscape offers diverse environments, from dense forests to impressive cliffs and panoramic viewpoints overlooking the water. Best family friendly hiking trails around Pantà de Susqueda The most popular family friendly hiking route is Enchanted Rocks – Coll de Condreu loop from Coll de Condreu, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This easy route features unique rock formations and forest paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Osor Village – View of Osor loop from Osor, a moderate 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This trail offers views of Osor village and its surrounding natural environment. Local hikers also love the Coll de Condreu – Enchanted Rocks loop from Coll de Condreu, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through varied forest and rock landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Hiking around Pantà de Susqueda is defined by reservoir views, dense woodlands, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, including more than 20 easy family friendly hiking trails. The routes in Pantà de Susqueda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 5,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Pantà de Susqueda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Pantà de Susqueda?
A: The Pantà de Susqueda area offers a wide variety of trails suitable for families. Our guide features over 70 routes, with more than 20 specifically rated as easy, making them perfect for family outings.

Q: What is the best time of year for family hiking around Pantà de Susqueda?
A: The region around Pantà de Susqueda is beautiful year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for hiking. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended, especially for trails with less shade. Winter provides a different, tranquil experience, but be prepared for cooler weather.

Q: Are there any easy, short walks suitable for families with young children?
A: Absolutely! For a gentle and engaging walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Rocks – Coll de Condreu loop from Coll de Condreu&lt;/a&gt;. This easy route covers just over 6 km and takes around 1 hour 50 minutes, offering a magical experience for kids.

Q: Can we bring our dog on the hiking trails around Pantà de Susqueda?
A: Yes, many trails around Pantà de Susqueda are dog-friendly. It's generally recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers, and always remember to clean up after your pet. The natural environment here is perfect for enjoying with your furry family members.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can we see along the family-friendly routes?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty and history. You can explore the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5566896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susqueda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself, or discover the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5533341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Querós&lt;/a&gt;. When water levels are low, remnants of the submerged village of Susqueda and its church can even emerge, offering a unique glimpse into the past.

Q: Are there any stroller-friendly or accessible routes for families?
A: While many trails in the mountainous terrain around Pantà de Susqueda involve uneven paths, some sections or wider tracks might be suitable for sturdy strollers. For easier access, look for routes described as 'easy' and check the terrain details on komoot's route planner. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31897434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Condreu – Enchanted Rocks loop from Coll de Condreu&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, though it's always best to check the specific path conditions for stroller suitability.

Q: Where can we find parking for family hikes near Pantà de Susqueda?
A: Parking is generally available at common starting points for trails, such as near the village of Osor or at Coll de Condreu. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions on komoot for recommended parking areas, especially during peak seasons, as spaces can fill up quickly.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pantà de Susqueda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.46 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic views of the reservoir and surrounding Guilleries Mountains, the tranquility of the natural environment, and the diverse trails suitable for various skill levels, including many family-friendly options.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, circular routes are excellent for families as they bring you back to your starting point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31897434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Condreu – Enchanted Rocks loop from Coll de Condreu&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of an easy, family-friendly circular trail, offering beautiful scenery without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of wildlife might we spot during our family hike?
A: The natural environment of Pantà de Susqueda is home to various bird species, making it an excellent spot for bird watching. You might also encounter other local wildlife, so keep an eye out and enjoy observing nature in its tranquil setting.

Q: Is public transport an option to reach the hiking trails around Pantà de Susqueda?
A: Access to the more remote hiking trailheads around Pantà de Susqueda is primarily by car. Public transport options are limited in this rural area, so planning your journey with a private vehicle is generally recommended for convenience and flexibility when hiking with family.
tips:
Una cascada espectacular, sobre una roca igualmente impresionante.

A spectacular waterfall, on an equally impressive rock.
The views from the Ter viewpoint are truly amazing. You will be able to see a panoramic view of the meander of the Ter. Personally, I think this is the best viewpoint of the River Ter.

The Susqueda swamp is a reservoir that belongs to the Ter river and is located in the Les Guilleries massif. The natural space it occupies is of great interest for its flora and fauna. Different water sports are practiced in it.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this circular route is rated as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It requires a very good physical condition and significant experience with mountain biking. You should be prepared for technical challenges, potentially corresponding to STS S3-S6, and there may be sections where you need to push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this challenging mountain biking route is approximately 5 hours and 13 minutes (18804 seconds), covering a distance of about 53.8 kilometers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved tracks (around 20.5 km), paved tracks (about 16.7 km), and natural paths or trails (approximately 16.4 km), some of which may be singletrack. Given the 'difficult' rating, expect varied surfaces, including rocky sections, steep ascents, and descents. Conditions can also vary with weather, potentially leading to mud or frozen patches.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers spectacular scenery and notable landmarks. Key highlights include the impressive Sallent Waterfall, Catalonia's largest vertical waterfall, and stunning panoramic views from the Viewpoint of the Ter River and Susqueda Reservoir and the Viewpoint of Susqueda Reservoir. You'll also traverse dense forests characteristic of the Guilleries massif.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in various seasons, but the Sallent Waterfall is particularly impressive after rainfall, when its flow is strongest. Be mindful of weather conditions, as wet or cold seasons can introduce mud or frozen patches, adding to the difficulty.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Space of Les Guilleries generally allows dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging nature of this specific mountain biking route and the presence of diverse wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, difficult ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the start of the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the exact start of this circular route isn't detailed, but common access points for the Pantà de Susqueda and Salt de Sallent areas often have designated parking. It's advisable to check local maps or community resources for the most convenient and safe parking options near the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in Les Guilleries?
faqs.answer: The Natural Space of Les Guilleries is a protected natural area. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the environment.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. Notable sections include parts of the Ruta del Ter, Camí de Sant Jaume (Sant Pere de Rodes - Montserrat), and Ruta d'enllaç Vilanova de Sau - Rupit, among others.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, appropriate safety gear including a helmet, gloves, and knee pads. Carry plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Weather-appropriate clothing is also crucial, as conditions can change in the mountains.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural, forested area, nearby towns like Rupit offer options for accommodation, cafes, and restaurants. It's a good idea to plan your food and drink stops in advance, especially for a long and demanding ride like this.
